title: Cheap houses for rent in Kamm's Corners
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in Kamm's Corners, Cleveland

secondaryKeywords: West Park Cleveland houses for rent
answerBox: Kamm's Corners is a commercial node in Cleveland's West Park section on the city's far west side. Citywide, Cleveland's median asking rent runs about $1,250 a month (Zumper, July 2026), with three-bedroom homes near $1,350; our own selection ranges roughly $700–$1,800, and every home we work with accepts Section 8 vouchers.

sections.h2: Renting a cheap house near Kamm's Corners
body: Kamm's Corners is a commercial node on Cleveland's far west side, part of the city's West Park section at the intersection of Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive. The wider West Park area also includes the Jefferson and Bellaire-Puritas neighborhoods, closer to the Cleveland Hopkins International Airport corridor.

Renters searching for a **cheap house** in this part of the city are usually weighing Cleveland's older housing stock — single-family homes and duplexes built in the early-to-mid 1900s — against the broader citywide rent market. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes across Greater Cleveland; our current inventory is concentrated on the city's East and Southeast sides, so [tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) in Kamm's Corners and the surrounding west side, and we'll let you know what's available or coming up.

sections.h2: How much does a cheap house cost in Cleveland right now?
body: As of July 2026, Cleveland's overall median asking rent is about $1,250 a month, up 4.2% year-over-year, per Zumper's July 2026 rent report; a three-bedroom home citywide runs closer to $1,350. RentCafe's July 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally managed apartment communities, shows a higher average of $1,564 for roughly 787 square feet — the gap reflects the difference between smaller, older listings and newer managed complexes, so it's worth checking both figures when budgeting for a lower-cost rental.

Across the 90+ rental homes we currently work with in Greater Cleveland, monthly rent typically runs from about $700 to $1,800, with a median near $1,000 — most are 2- and 3-bedroom homes, and every one accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8).

sections.h2: Getting around the West Park area
body: West Park is served by GCRTA bus routes running along Lorain Avenue and connecting into the wider [regional transit network](https://www.riderta.com/routes), and Cleveland Hopkins International Airport sits nearby on the city's far southwest side. Interstate 71 runs along Cleveland's west side, giving West Park renters a direct route toward Downtown's Tower City rail hub, where GCRTA's Red Line, Blue Line, Green Line, and Waterfront Line all converge.

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly houses near Kamm's Corners
body: Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) are administered locally by the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), which accepts preliminary voucher applications year-round through its [online applicant portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland itself does not have a source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance varies by landlord. Every home we work with is Section 8-friendly and HUD-inspection-ready — read more in our [Cleveland Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

If a specific Kamm's Corners address isn't available when you're ready to look, nearby west-side neighborhoods such as [Old Brooklyn](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-old-brooklyn-cleveland/) and [Detroit-Shoreway](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-detroit-shoreway-cleveland/) are part of our current service area, alongside a larger concentration of homes on Cleveland's East and Southeast sides. Browse our full list of [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to compare neighborhoods and price points.

Because we work with the same 90+ homes across all of these areas from one leasing team, sharing your budget, bedroom count, and preferred side of town lets us match you against current and upcoming listings without you having to check a dozen separate neighborhood pages one at a time.

Tell our team what you're looking for in Kamm's Corners and we'll let you know about current or upcoming availability nearby.

faq.q: What's the average rent for a house in Cleveland?
faq.a: As of July 2026, Zumper reports Cleveland's median asking rent at about $1,250 a month, with three-bedroom homes near $1,350. Across the 90+ homes we work with, rent typically ranges from about $700 to $1,800, with a median close to $1,000, and every home accepts Housing Choice Vouchers.

faq.q: Do Cleveland landlords near Kamm's Corners accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Acceptance varies by landlord — Ohio has no statewide law requiring it, and the City of Cleveland does not have a source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of neighborhood.

faq.q: What's the housing stock like in the West Park area?
faq.a: West Park, which includes Kamm's Corners, Jefferson, and Bellaire-Puritas, is a far-west-side section of Cleveland near the Cleveland Hopkins International Airport corridor, with a mix of early-to-mid-1900s single-family homes and duplexes typical of the city's older neighborhoods.

faq.a: Availability changes frequently across our 90+ managed homes. The fastest way to get a current answer for Kamm's Corners or nearby west-side neighborhoods is to contact our leasing team directly with your budget and bedroom needs, and we'll point you to what's open now or coming soon.
